channel.title: Aaron's Blog
channel.description: Hi, I am Aaron Seongjoon Cho, offensive security researcher from Republic of Korea.

item.title: Escaping V8 Sandbox via RegExp Bytecode (Chromium < 125.0.6422.60)
item.description: In this post, I will explain how to escape V8 sandbox to get RCE via RegExp bytecode, while we have limited exploitation pri
item.content:encoded:
In this post, I will explain how to escape V8 sandbox to get RCE via RegExp bytecode, while we have limited exploitation primitives like addrof and sandboxed AAR/AAW.

Setup

  • Ubuntu 24.04.1 LTS (WSL)
  • 0c1231b6414d19468d6f7a35ff5b6167626f57a5 (Mar 22nd, 2024)

Run setup.zsh in your working directory.

Analysis

RegExp bytecode

RegExp object is used for matching text with regular expression, and it is implemented as JSRegExp class.

When JSRegExp object is constructed, data array is initialized.

Data array is consisted of 12 fields. Among them, code in 4th field and bytecode in 6th field store the code which is executed when exec() method is called, and they are initialized to kUninitializedValue (blue box). bytecode is compiled when exec() is called for the first time, and code is compiled after exec() is called a certain number (regexp_tier_up_ticks) of times. TierUpTick in 11th field is initialized to regexp_tier_up_ticks (green box), and decreased by 1 each time exec() is called. When TierupTick reaches to 0, tier-up is proceeded and code is compiled.

regexp_tier_up and regexp_tier_up_ticks can be controlled by run-time options.

exec() is handled by RUNTIME_FUNCTION(Runtime_RegExpExec). Before tier-up, It goes into RegExpExec() → RegExp::Exec() → RegExpImpl::IrregexpExec() → RegExpImpl::IrregexpExecRaw() → IrregexpInterpreter::MatchForCallFromRuntime() → IrregexpInterpreter::Match() → IrregexpInterpreter::MatchInternal() → RawMatch(), and bytecode is executed in RawMatch().

Exploitation

Stack buffer overflow in RawMatch()

Array in bytecode field of data array is inside of V8 sandbox, so we can arbitrarily edit bytecode using sandboxed AAW primitive, which means that we can execute arbitrary bytecode.

Both of virtual registers and backtrack_stack used while executing bytecode are allocated in stack frame of RawMatch(). Bytecode accesses to the values in registers via their index. However, there is no bounds check when accessing to registers, thus we can read and write out of bounds of the stack frame. This would be stack buffer overflow, so we can just do ROP and get shell.

Get binary base address and stack address

We can get binary base address of d8 from the return address of RawMatch() (red box), and get stack address from the stack frame pointer of RawMatch() (blue box) stored at rbp. Then, we can use the gadgets in d8 binary and stack address for ROP.

Execute command via execve

We can execute arbitrary shell command by writing arbitrary command (e.g. /bin/sh) not to be overlapped with ROP chain and passing it to the first argument of execve system call.

pwn.js

Patch

[regex] Check bounds on register access in interpreter (Mar 22nd, 2024)
Registers in the interpreter are stored on the stack/heap outside the sandbox. The register index is stored in the bytecode, which is (not yet) in truested space.

References

  • V8 Sandbox escape via regexp - Chromium Issues
  • V8-Sandbox-Escape-via-Regexp - rycbar77 (Github)

item.title: Issue 41483297 (Type Confusion in V8)
item.description: One day, I noticed that Issue 40069798 which was a type confusion bug in V8 was
item.content:encoded:
One day, I noticed that Issue 40069798 which was a type confusion bug in V8 was disclosed. This bug was awarded $15,000 for renderer RCE, but no CVE number was assigned because it was living in experimental feature and only could be triggered under --harmony or --harmony-set-methods flag in V8. While studying this bug, I tried fuzzing targeting Harmony Set methods in which it was living, with a light heart. Not long after, I unexpectedly got a same DCHECK failing crash, and could successfully achieve RCE in the same way. In this post, I will analyze and exploit my bug, Issue 41483297.

Before reading, I recommend you to read my post for Issue 40069798 first, because I will omit many overlapping contents in here.

Setup

  • Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS
  • 1976a3f375fb686a12d0577b0a01b164d8481414 (Dec 12th, 2023)

Run setup.zsh in your working directory.

Analysis

Root cause

Set.prototype.symmetricDifference() method is handled by SetPrototypeSymmetricDifference(). It calls GetKeysIterator() to get keysIter which is iterator of other, after getting table of receiver. GetKeysIterator() internally calls keys() method of other. We can let arbitrary JavaScript code be executed at this point, by redefining keys() method of other. If we make table of receiver to be grown or shrunk by adding or deleting element, new table is allocated and its address is stored in the size field of old table. However, corrupted old table is used as it is after GetKeysIterator() is returned. It is set to table of new Set which is returned to us, going through resultSetData, resultAndNumberOfElements.setData, and shrunk. This can cause type confusion.

Proof of concept

poc.js

We can see that the address of table of a (blue box) is stored in the size field of table of s (green box). Thus, we can get an OrderedHashSet object which is table of a, by reading s.size.

Bisection

[set-methods]Getting other before receiver’s table (Aug 18th, 2023)

This CL fix the issue of clearing receiver in case of having user arbitraty code in the other.

The bug has been existed since symmetricDifference() method was added, but was exposed when the previous bug was patched in the commit above.

Patch

[set-methods]Get receiver’s table after other’s keys() (Dec 12th, 2023)

This CL fixes the issue of clearing receiver’s table in case of having user’s arbitrary code in other’s keys().

Exploitation

shellcode.py pwn.wat wasm.py

You have to install /bin/xcalc by running sudo apt install -y x11-apps before executing pwn.js if you are using WSL.

References

  • Security: Type confusion in Harmony Set methods (Leads to RCE) - Chromium Issues

item.title: Escaping V8 Sandbox via WebAssembly JIT Spraying: Part 2 (110.0.5481.77 <= Chromium < 122.0.6261.57)
item.content:encoded:
After the patch for the exploitation technique explained in part 1, we cannot directly overwrite call target and control rip. However, a new technique was introduced because of newly implemented lazy compilation for Wasm function. I will explain about this in this post.

Setup

  • Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS (WSL)
  • 8cf17a14a78cc1276eb42e1b4bb699f705675530 (Jan 4th, 2024)

Run setup.zsh in your working directory.

Analysis

WebAssembly lazy compilation

When a Wasm module is constructed, a memory region whose permission is set to RWX is allocated for compiling and executing Wasm functions.

There is jump table at the very front of this region, which is responsible for deciding execution flow when Wasm function is called.

When a function is called for the first time, it jumps to Builtins_WasmCompileLazy() and compile the function lazily in run-time.

From the second call, it directly jumps to compiled code.

Builtins_WasmCompileLazy() is generate by Builtins::Generate_WasmCompileLazy(). It adds code which set r15 to jump_table_start in WasmInstanceObject and jumps to r15. As a result, generated code of Builtins_WasmCompileLazy() looks like following:

Exploitation

Hijack execution flow

With lazy compilation enabled, we can overwrite jump_table_start with the address which we want to jump to, before Wasm function is compiled. Then, Builtins_WasmCompileLazy() will jump to the address after compilation is over, which means that we can control rip.

shellcode.py pwn.wat wasm.py

You have to install /bin/xcalc by running sudo apt install -y x11-apps before executing pwn.js if you are using WSL.

Bisection

[wasm] Enable lazy compilation by default (Nov 14th, 2022)

The new exploitation technique explained in this post was introduced in the commit above, which enabled lazy compilation for Wasm function by default.

Patch

[wasm] Introduce WasmTrustedInstanceData (Jan 4th, 2024)
This CL moves most data from the WasmInstanceObject to a new WasmTrustedInstanceData. As the name suggests, this new object is allocated in the trusted space and can hence hold otherwise-unsafe data (like direct pointers). As the Wasm instance was still storing some unsafe pointers, this CL closes holes in the V8 sandbox, and allows us to land follow-up refactorings to remove more indirections for sandboxing (potentially after moving more data structures to the trusted space).

item.title: Issue 40069798 (Type Confusion in V8)
item.description: In this post, I will analyze and exploit Issue 40069798. Even though this is a
item.content:encoded:
In this post, I will analyze and exploit Issue 40069798. Even though this is a type confusion bug which leads to RCE, no CVE number was assigned to this bug because it was living in experimental feature and only could be triggered under --harmony or --harmony-set-methods flag in V8.

Setup

  • Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS
  • f118dd45b7cde0d320e022068f0e98dbc53b2ba9 (Aug 17th, 2023)

Run setup.zsh in your working directory.

Analysis

ECMAScript Harmony

ECMAScript - Wikipedia
ECMAScript (ES) is a standard for scripting languages, including JavaScript, JScript, and ActionScript. It is best known as a JavaScript standard intended to ensure the interoperability of web pages across different web browsers. It is standardized by Ecma International in the document ECMA-262.

The first edition of ECMA-262 was published in June 1997, and it is being published in June every year from 6th edition which is published on June 2015.

Ecma International approves major revision of ECMAScript - Ecma International
The last major revision of the ECMAScript standard was the Third Edition, published in 1999. After completion of the Third Edition, significant work was done to develop a Fourth Edition. Although development of a Fourth Edition was not completed, that work influenced ECMAScript, Fifth Edition and is continuing to influence the ongoing development of ECMAScript. Work on future ECMAScript editions continues as part of the previously announced ECMAScript Harmony project.

We can use Harmony features in V8 by enabling run-time option --harmony, etc.

Set

Set is a collection of unique values.

Elements of JSSet object are stored in OrderedHashSet, which is OrderedHashTable whose entrysize is 1.

Set has following set composition methods, which figure out the relationship with another Set.

  • difference()
  • intersection()
  • symmetricDifference()
  • union()
  • isDisjointFrom()
  • isSubsetOf()
  • isSupersetOf()

Root cause

Every methods of Set start with following code in common.

They call GetSetRecord() to get otherRec which is iterator of other, after getting table of receiver. GetSetRecord() internally calls GetProperty() to get size property of other. We can let arbitrary JavaScript code be executed at this point, by redefining size property of other as a getter. If we make table of receiver to be grown or shrunk by adding or deleting element, new table is allocated and its address is stored in the size field of old table. However, corrupted old table is used as it is after GetSetRecord() is returned. This can cause type confusion.

Proof of concept

Calling any method except for union() in poc1.js lets DCHECK fail.

In case of union(), corrupted table becomes resultSetData, and it is stored in a new Set which is returned by union(). In poc2.js, There is the address of table of a in the size field of table of u.

u.size gets the value stored in the size field of table, so we can directly access to table of a, which is an OrderedHashSet object.

Bisection

[set-methods] Add feature flag and union method (May 30th, 2023)
[set-methods] Add intersection to set methods (Jun 14th, 2023)
[set-methods] Add difference to set methods (Jun 23rd, 2023)
[set-methods] Add symmetricDifference (Jun 28th, 2023)
[set-methods] Add isSubsetOf method (Jul 27th, 2023)
[set-methods] Add isSupersetOf method (Jul 28th, 2023)
[set-methods] Add isDisjointFrom to set methods (Aug 1st, 2023)

The bug has been existed since each Set method was added.

Patch

[set-methods]Getting other before receiver’s table (Aug 18th, 2023)
This CL fix the issue of clearing receiver in case of having user arbitraty code in the other.

Exploitation

Generate fake object

We can increase or decrease the value stored in the size field of corrupted table by adding or deleting elements. It means that we can control the address of the object which we can get by u.size in poc2.js. As a result, we can get fake object at the address.

Implement sandboxed AAR/AAW primitive

We don’t have any leak, but still we can generate float_arr which is fake array of float numbers, because both of map and properties are almost constants. We can set elements to arbitrary address, then we can access to the address and read or write value. These would be sandboxed AAR/AAW primitives.

Implement addrof primitive

Now we can read or write value at arbitrary address, but we don’t know where to read or write because we still don’t have any leak.

The address of JavaScript object is random, but memory region for JavaScript objects stands still.

We can insert a marker to the float_arr_struct, and scan for this region to find it. If we meet the marker while scanning, we can calculate the address of float_arr.

However, if we scan for full memory region, our exploitation is gonna be broken because of garbage collection. We can experientially know that the address of any object is never lower than certain value, 0x4e000 in my case, so we can just start scanning from there.

If we define float_arr_struct and obj_arr consecutively in size getter, we can calculate the address of obj_arr using the address of float_arr which we found by scanning for memory, because the distance between them is invariant. Then, reading obj_arr[0] using sandboxed AAR primitive, we can get the address of the object stored in obj_arr[0] as a float number. This would be addrof primitive.

Escape V8 sandbox to get RCE

Escaping V8 Sandbox via WebAssembly JIT Spraying: Part 2 (110.0.5481.77 <= Chromium < 122.0.6261.57)

shellcode.py pwn.wat wasm.py

You have to install /bin/xcalc by running sudo apt install -y x11-apps before executing pwn.js if you are using WSL.

References

  • Security: CSA_DCHECK failed: Torque assert ‘Is<A>(o)’ failed - Chromium Issues

item.title: CVE-2023-2033 (Type Confusion in V8)
item.description: In this post, I will analyze and exploit CVE-2023-2033, an in-the-wild type confusion bug in V8. Using this bug, we can leak hole</
item.content:encoded:
In this post, I will analyze and exploit CVE-2023-2033, an in-the-wild type confusion bug in V8. Using this bug, we can leak hole object and exploit it to get RCE.

Setup

  • Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS (WSL)
  • f7a3499f6d7e50b227a17d2bbd96e4b59a261d3c (Apr 12th, 2023)

Run setup.zsh in your working directory.

Analysis

Stack trace API

When a new Error object is generated, it basically has two properties, error_stack_symbol which contains captured stack trace, and its accessor stack.

error_stack_symbol is an array consisted of CallSiteInfos, while each CallSiteInfo represents a stack frame in stack trace.

The maximum size of error_stack_symbol is controlled by run-time option --stack-trace-limit (default: 10).

When we access to accessor stack for the first time, all CallSiteInfos are collected and reconstructed as a string.

The stack tracing mechanism shown above is implemented using stack trace API which is also available to user scripts.

With Error.captureStackTrace(), we can capture stack trace and save it to arbitrary object.

Redefining Error.prepareStackTrace(), we can customize the way that stack trace is returned to user. When we access to accessor stack for the first time, Error.prepareStackTrace() is called and its return value is saved to error_stack_symbol.

Global property access optimization

Global property means property of JSGlobalObject. We can add a global property by defining a variable using var keyword, or adding a property to globalThis object, which represents JSGlobalProxy, a Proxy object for JSGlobalObject. In JavaScript, we can’t directly access to JSGlobalObject, but can access via JSGlobalProxy.

Properties of JSGlobalObject are managed by GlobalDictionary, as a form of PropertyCell.

When Turbofan compiles a function, global property access is handled by JSNativeContextSpecialization::ReduceGlobalAccess().

When access_mode is AccessMode::kLoad, which means that the function is trying to read global property, the address of property cell is optimized to a constant if following conditions are satisfied:

  1. !property_details.IsConfigurable() && property_details.IsReadOnly() is false
  2. property_details.cell_type() == PropertyCellType::kConstant || property_details.cell_type() == PropertyCellType::kUndefined is false

We can see that the code of f() is optimized to read gvar from the constant property cell (red box).

On the other hand, we also can see the code for lazy deoptimization (blue box). At this point, rbx has the address of Code object, and there are FLAGS_BIT_FIELDS in rbx+0x17.

The optimized code checks if 29th bit of FLAGS_BIT_FIELDS is on, which means MarkedForDeoptimizationField is set to true. If so, it calls Builtins_CompileLazyDeoptimizedCode() to deoptimize the function.

Code dependency and deoptimization

When Turbofan compiles a function, it installs code dependency to the optimized code. Code dependency means some prerequisites that should be met for normal operation of the optimized code. If code dependency is broken, deoptimization should be proceeded.

DependentCode is a list consisted of tuples of Code object and DependencyGroup. If a Code object is in certain DependencyGroup, it means that the code should be deoptimized if the code dependency for that DependencyGroup is broken.

When delete gvar is executed, the_hole goes into the property cell of gvar. The optimized code of f() shouldn’t work after deleting gvar, because f() was optimized to read gvar from the constant property cell, which now contains the_hole.

Code objects in kPropertyCellChangedGroup depend on global property values in property cells not being changed, but this dependency is broken because we deleted gvar.

After calling code.SetMarkedForDeoptimization(), MarkedForDeoptimizationField of code is set to true, so f() will be deoptimized on next call.

Root cause

If we try to modify the value of stack right after calling Error.captureStackTrace(), we can see that the value is set to 2 after Error.prepareStackTrace() is completely finished.

Applying this, we can write following code:

When we try to set configurable descriptor of stack to true using Object.defineProperty() right after calling Error.captureStackTrace(), Error.prepareStackTrace() is called first because it is the first access to stack.

In Error.prepareStackTrace(), configurable descriptor of stack is set to false.

However, it is set to true again, following the original purpose of Object.defineProperty(). This is definitely not normal but a bug.

Proof of concept

We set configurable descriptor of global property stack from false to true using the bug. We optimize a function which reads the value of stack while configurable is false, which allows us to avoid installing code dependency for kPropertyCellChangedGroup. After that, we set configurable to true and delete stack. Then, the_hole will go into the property cell of stack, but the function will not be deoptimized because there is no code dependency. In conclusion, we can leak the_hole from the property cell of stack.

poc.js

Patch

[runtime] Make Error.captureStackTrace() a no-op for global object (Apr 12th, 2023)

Exploitation

Exploiting V8 Hole via Number Constructor (Chromium < 115.0.5790.98)

You have to install /bin/xcalc by running sudo apt install -y x11-apps before executing pwn.js if you are using WSL.

References

  • Stable Channel Update for Desktop (Friday, April 14, 2023) - Chrome Releases
  • Security: [0-day] JIT optimisation issue - Chromium Issues
  • mistymntncop/CVE-2023-2033 - Github
  • Stack trace API - V8 Docs

item.title: Exploiting V8 Hole via Number Constructor (Chromium < 115.0.5790.98)
item.description: In this post, I will explain how to exploit leaked hole object in V8 for getting RCE, via Number() constructor.
item.content:encoded:
In this post, I will explain how to exploit leaked hole object in V8 for getting RCE, via Number() constructor.

Setup

  • Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS
  • fdc017c89bf910e16f1fa5c6c16022e9e019c6a1 (May 17th, 2023)

Save dcheck.diff and setup.zsh in your working directory and run setup.zsh.

Exploitation

Leak hole

Using % TheHole(), we can simulate a bug which allows us to leak hole object.

OOB read

The main idea of this technique is applying Number() constructor to hole and optimize it via Turbofan.

When we pass hole as the argument, Number() returns NaN.

When Turbofan compiles a function, Number() corresponds to JSToNumberConvertBigInt node, and it is typed by OperationTyper::ToNumberConvertBigInt().

Before ToNumber() is called, type is Hole.

The return value of OperationTyper::ToNumberConvertBigInt(), which means the type of JSToNumberConvertBigInt node, is None.

None means no type, so None is ignored if we union None with other type using ternary operator.

The type of bool ? the.hole : -1 can be both of the type of the.hole which is Hole, and the type of -1 which is Range(-1, -1). However, Hole is converted to None at JSToNumberConvertBigInt node and ignored, so the type of the return value of f() is Range(-1, -1), even though it returns NaN.

Applying this, we can write oob.js which performs out of bounds read.

If we use arr1[idx] instead of arr1.at(idx), kAbortOnOutOfBounds flag is set which leads to SIGTRAP when OOB access is detected. In case of arr1.at(idx), it just checks if index is always lower than length from the viewpoint of Turbofan, so we can successfully achieve OOB read, but not OOB write unfortunately.

Implement addrof/fakeobj primitives

We can allocate array of float numbers and array of objects consecutively, and trigger OOB read in array of float numbers. Then, we can read the address of object which is stored in array of objects and get it as a float number. This would be addrof primitive.

In reverse, we can allocate array of objects and array of float numbers consecutively, and trigger OOB read in array of objects. Then, we can read the arbitrary address stored in array of float numbers and get it as a fake object. This would be fakeobj primitive.

Implement sandboxed AAR/AAW primitives

We can generate a fake array of float numbers using fakeobj primitive, and set the address of elements array of this array to arbitrary address. If so, we can access to arbitrary address inside of V8 sandbox via this fake array, and read or write arbitrary 8-byte value. These would be sandboxed AAR/AAW primitives.

Escape V8 sandbox to get RCE

Escaping V8 Sandbox via Turbofan JIT Spraying (100.0.4896.60 <= Chromium < 117.0.5938.62)

You have to install /bin/xcalc by running sudo apt install -y x11-apps before executing pwn.js if you are using WSL.

Patch

Introduce a dedicated type for the_hole (May 17th, 2023)
Previously, the_hole was an Oddball object (like ‘null’, ‘undefined’, or ‘true’/‘false’) and therefore it was possible to perform certain operations on the_hole, such as ToNumber conversion. This should
probably not be possible, so this CL now introduces a dedicated Hole object type. In the future, this will also allow introducing special-purpose holes that only have special meaning in one context.

item.title: Exploiting V8 Hole via Corrupting Map Object (Chromium < 103.0.5060.53)
item.description: In this post, I will explain how to exploit leaked hole object in V8 to get RCE, via corrupting Map object.
item.content:encoded:
In this post, I will explain how to exploit leaked hole object in V8 to get RCE, via corrupting Map object.

Setup

  • Ubuntu 20.04.6 LTS (WSL)
  • dbff30456e14ec3fec54869b3e41d091d2489461 (Apr 27th, 2022)

Save dcheck.diff and setup.zsh in your working directory, and run setup.zsh.

Analysis

Map

Map object is collection of key-value pairs.

Elements of Map are stored in OrderedHashMap, which remembers the order of elements. OrderedHashMap inherits OrderedHashTable, which is basically a FixedArray, so all data are stored linearly.

From the start, the number of stored elements, the number of deleted elements, and the number of buckets are stored. Capacity is the maximum number of elements. The number of elements that each bucket can hold is kLoadFactor, so capacity is kLoadFactor multiplied by the number of buckets.

Right after, there is hash table which contains the indices of elements which are lastly added to each bucket.

Each element is stored in data table, as a triplet consisted of key, value, and the index of previous element in corresponding bucket.

If the number of elements are over capacity, extended table is newly allocated by SmallOrderedHashSet::Grow(), and the address of new table is stored in old table.

If the number of elements is less than a quarter of capacity, reduced table is newly allocated by OrderedHashTable<Derived, entrysize>::Rehash(), and the address of new table is stored in old table.

Key and value of deleted elements are set to hole.

Exploitation

Leak hole

Using % TheHole(), we can simulate a bug which allows us to leak hole object.

Corrupt Map using hole

When we delete an element from Map, key and value of that element are set to hole in OrderedHashMap. If we delete an element whose key is hole in this state, the element at the same index is deleted once again.

The size of map is 0 now, even though one element which we added at first still remains. If we delete this element,

We can see the size of map is set to -1 (red box).

It means that the element we add next will be stored at the position corresponding to index -1 (blue box). Key of the element corresponds to the number of buckets, and value of the element corresponds to the index of lastly added element of the first bucket.

Generate OOB array

If we allocate an array of float numbers right behind corrupted map, we can see following memory layout:

Overwriting the number of buckets of map (red box) with a bigger value, we can extend hash table of map to right before length field of oob_arr (blue box). Then, (fake) data table starts from the length field of oob_arr, so the key of the element which we add to map next will be the new length of oob_arr, just if it goes into the right bucket without SIGSEGV. Instead of calculating hash, we can just do a little brute force.

Escape V8 sandbox to get RCE

Escaping V8 Sandbox via Turbofan JIT Spraying (100.0.4896.60 <= Chromium < 117.0.5938.62)

You have to install /bin/xcalc by running sudo apt install -y x11-apps before executing pwn.js if you are using WSL.

Patch

Harden Map.prototype.delete and related methods (Apr 27th, 2022)
These can be tricked into corrupting memory when an attacker can leak the “hole” value due to a bug. This CL simply adds CHECKs to prevent this. A longer-term solution might be to introduce “special-purpose holes” so that a leaked “hole” value can no longer be used to confuse unrelated code like the JSMap implementation because that would then use a different “hole” value.

item.title: Escaping V8 Sandbox via Turbofan JIT Spraying (100.0.4896.60 <= Chromium < 117.0.5938.62)
item.description: In this post, I will explain how to escape V8 sandbox to get RCE via Turbofan JIT spraying, while we have limited exploitation primitives
item.content:encoded:
In this post, I will explain how to escape V8 sandbox to get RCE via Turbofan JIT spraying, while we have limited exploitation primitives like addrof and sandboxed AAR/AAW.

Setup

  • Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS (WSL)
  • 4512c6eb7189c21f39420ddf8d9ff4f05a4a39b4 (Jul 11th, 2023)

Run setup.zsh in your working directory.

Exploitation

Implement exploitation primitives

We can implement sandboxed addrof primitive which returns address of arbitrary JavaScript object in V8 sandbox, using Sandbox.getAddressOf. Also, we can implement sandboxed AAR/AAW primitives which read and write value at arbitrary address in V8 sandbox, using Sandbox.MemoryView.

JIT (Just-In-Time) spraying

jit() is a function which returns an array consisted of float numbers. After jit() is compiled via Turbofan, the numbers in returned array are inserted to the optimized code as raw numbers. It means that we can insert arbitrary 8-byte numbers to executable code region.

When jit() is called, rip moves to instruction_start pointer in code of jit(), which is inside of V8 sandbox. Using sandboxed AAW primitive, we can overwrite instruction_start pointer with arbitrary value, e.g. the address of number which we inserted to the optimized code. If so, the number acts like an 8-byte shellcode.

Construct shellcode chain

With just 8-byte shellcode, we won’t be able to do what we want to do. Instead, we can chain several short shellcodes using jmp instruction.

shellcode.py

If there are same numbers in the array which jit() returns, the optimized code remembers that number in register and reuse it later. If so, chain is broken and shellcode doesn’t work. Therefore, we should slightly change the order of instructions or move the position of nop instructions to make sure that all numbers are different, like following:

You have to install /bin/xcalc by running sudo apt install -y x11-apps before executing pwn.js if you are using WSL.

Bisection

[ext-code-space] Enable external code space on x64 and desktop arm64 (Jan 24th, 2022)

v8_enable_external_code_space was set to true in x64 in the commit above, so code_entry_point in CodeDataContainer became available. As a result, the exploitation technique explained in this post was introduced.

Patch

[sandbox] Enable code pointer sandboxing (Jul 11th, 2023)
Revert “[sandbox] Enable code pointer sandboxing” (Jul 11th, 2023)
Reland “[sandbox] Enable code pointer sandboxing” (Jul 11th, 2023)

item.title: Escaping V8 Sandbox via WebAssembly JIT Spraying: Part 1 (Chromium < 106.0.5249.61)
item.description: In this post, I will explain how to escape V8 sandbox to get RCE via WebAssembly JIT spraying, while we have limited exploitation primiti
item.content:encoded:
In this post, I will explain how to escape V8 sandbox to get RCE via WebAssembly JIT spraying, while we have limited exploitation primitives like addrof and sandboxed AAR/AAW.

Setup

  • Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS
  • cb5c1b8a1fd1eee214501ee06fdd4566886803c1 (Jul 26th, 2022)

Run setup.zsh in your working directory.

Analysis

WebAssembly jump table

When a Wasm module is constructed, a memory region whose permission is set to RWX is allocated for compiling and executing Wasm functions.

There is jump table at the very front of this region, which is responsible for deciding execution flow when Wasm function is called.

Each jmp instruction in jump table is pointing the address of compiled code of each function in Wasm module.

The address of jump table is stored in WasmInstanceObject, as jump_table_start.

Exploitation

Implement exploitation primitives

We can implement sandboxed addrof primitive which returns address of arbitrary JavaScript object in V8 sandbox, using Sandbox.getAddressOf. Also, we can implement sandboxed AAR/AAW primitives which read and write value at arbitrary address in V8 sandbox, using Sandbox.MemoryView.

JIT (Just-In-Time) spraying

main() in wasm_module is a function which loads 64-bit constants. These constants are inserted to the compiled code as raw numbers. It means that we can insert arbitrary 8-byte constants to executable code region. If we move rip to the address of any constant, it will act like an 8-byte shellcode.

Construct shellcode chain

With just 8-byte shellcode, we won’t be able to do what we want to do. Instead, we can chain several short shellcodes using jmp instruction.

shellcode.py

If main() loads the same number twice, the second is not inserted to compiled code. Then, chain is broken and shellcode doesn’t work. Therefore, we should slightly change the order of instructions or move the position of nop instructions to make sure that all numbers are different. In addition, we should consider that the distance between shellcodes is changed from the middle because the code which backs registers up to stack is inserted if all registers are in use.

pwn.wat wasm.py

Hijack execution flow

When Wasm function is called, we can see following execution flow:

WasmInternalFunction object is inside of V8 sandbox, so we can overwrite its call target using sandboxed AAW primitive and control rip.

pwn.js

Patch

[sandbox] Refactor and sandboxify WasmInternalFunction::call_target (Jul 26th, 2022)
This CL refactors WasmInternalFunction to no longer inherit from Foreign but instead contain a (sandboxed) ExternalPointer field for the call target.

item.title: Escaping V8 Sandbox via ArrayBuffer and WebAssembly Jump Table (Chromium < 100.0.4896.60)
item.description: In this post, I will explain how to escape V8 sandbox to get RCE via ArrayBuffer and WebAssembly jump table, while we have limited exploi
item.content:encoded:
In this post, I will explain how to escape V8 sandbox to get RCE via ArrayBuffer and WebAssembly jump table, while we have limited exploitation primitives like addrof and sandboxed AAR/AAW.

Setup

  • Ubuntu 20.04.6 LTS (WSL)
  • 7c369ec82136ac0afc559aaa0b31614840fcc0a0 (Feb 15th, 2022)

Save sandbox.diff and setup.zsh in your working directory, and run setup.zsh (zsh is required).

sandbox.diff is the same patch with [sandbox] Add new Memory Corruption API (May 20th, 2022). Applying this patch, we can implement limited exploitation primitives using memory corruption API.

Exploitation

Implement exploitation primitives

We can implement sandboxed addrof primitive which returns address of any JavaScript object in V8 sandbox, using Sandbox.getAddressOf. Also, we can implement sandboxed AAR/AAW primitives which read and write value at arbitrary address in V8 sandbox, using Sandbox.MemoryView.

Expand AAR/AAW

ArrayBuffer stores data in its backing store.

The address of backing store is saved in ArrayBuffer object as 8-byte full pointer. We can overwrite this pointer with arbitrary address using sandboxed AAW primitive. Then, the value we store in this ArrayBuffer will be written to the new backing store. That would be full AAR/AAW primitives, which work outside of V8 sandbox.

Execute shellcode

When we construct a Wasm module, a memory region with RWX permission is allocated for compiling and executing Wasm function.

At the head of this region, there is jump table which is responsible for deciding execution flow of Wasm function. In short, when Wasm function is called, rip moves to the jump table. So, if we overwrite jump table with arbitrary shellcode using full AAW primitive, the shellcode will be executed when Wasm function is called.

You have to install /bin/xcalc by running sudo apt install -y x11-apps before executing pwn.js if you are using WSL.

Patch

[wasm] Ship code protection via memory protection keys (Feb 15th, 2022)
